scenario_content:
[Issue Title]: Unable to connect to IMAP via SSL/TLS

[Post 1]:
Expected behavior
During incoming mail settings validation k9mail connects to IMAP server via SSL/TLS, requests user to accept unmatching certificate, and accepts incoming mail settings.
Actual behavior
Validation of incoming mail settings fails with "unable to connect", unclear what the problem is, no possibility to accept unmatching certificate.
Steps to reproduce

Add new account
Configure incoming mail with SSL/TLS
Hit Next

Environment
K-9 Mail version: 5.403
Android version: 7.0
Account type (IMAP, POP3, WebDAV/Exchange): IMAP
Note
The same procedure DOES work using "no encryption".
The same procedure DOES work for outgoing mail using SMTP: when validating settings, confirmation dialog popped up, I accepted the certificate, and the validation succeeded.
I also imported the server's certificate into Android's personal certificate storage, did not help either.
The problem happened after the certificate was renewed on the server.
The certificate (*.domain.com) does not exactly match to the server name (mail.domain.com) in k9mail.

[Post 2]:
What algorithms does the server support?
Most likely the answer is very few.
The problem with TLS errors is if the server doesn't like any of the algorithms we send it doesn't give you a helpful error message. So there's nothing we can put to help diagnose the problem.

[Post 3]:
Closing as duplicate of #1789

[Post 4]:
This is the answer I received from the server support, might be useful, so I add it here.

server ... no longer accept SMTP connections except if they are over TLS1.2 (TLS1.1, TLS1.0 as well as any version of SSL are no longer supported; SSL itself is obsolete and insecure and has been superseded by its replacement, TLS, which is an improvement of and development upon SSL - which should no longer be used for security reasons) and use one of the following encryption cypher / hashing algorithm sets :
ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384
ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384
ECDHE-ECDSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305
ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305
ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256
ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256
ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-SHA384
ECDHE-RSA-AES256-SHA384
ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-SHA256
ECDHE-RSA-AES128-SHA256
Versions of TLS older than 1.2 as well as all versions of SSL are obsolete and should no longer be considered secure or be used.
If your phone or device is running Android Nougat then the issue is with your device's operating system and whatever mail app you try to use will most likely not be able to connect to our server. This is due to Android Nougat not support elliptic curve encryption or only supporting obsolete methods of elliptic curve encryption or something along those lines.
More information on the issue with Android Nougat that's at the heart of this situation can be found at :
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/39133437/sslhandshakeexception-handshake-failed-on-android-n-7-0
and
https://code.google.com/p/android/issues/detail?id=224438 .
system_prompt:
你是一位经验丰富的软件工程师和项目管理者，专注于软件系统中的价值风险分析。
你的任务是分析给定的GitHub Issue讨论内容，识别其中涉及的人类价值关切。

价值风险是指软件系统可能对用户或利益相关者的重要价值（如隐私、安全、公平性、可用性等）产生负面影响的潜在问题。
Issue讨论中可能包含用户对软件行为的不满、功能请求、Bug报告等，这些内容可能隐含对某些价值的关切。
user_prompt:
## 价值场景（Issue讨论）
[Issue Title]: Unable to connect to IMAP via SSL/TLS

[Post 1]:
Expected behavior
During incoming mail settings validation k9mail connects to IMAP server via SSL/TLS, requests user to accept unmatching certificate, and accepts incoming mail settings.
Actual behavior
Validation of incoming mail settings fails with "unable to connect", unclear what the problem is, no possibility to accept unmatching certificate.
Steps to reproduce

Add new account
Configure incoming mail with SSL/TLS
Hit Next

Environment
K-9 Mail version: 5.403
Android version: 7.0
Account type (IMAP, POP3, WebDAV/Exchange): IMAP
Note
The same procedure DOES work using "no encryption".
The same procedure DOES work for outgoing mail using SMTP: when validating settings, confirmation dialog popped up, I accepted the certificate, and the validation succeeded.
I also imported the server's certificate into Android's personal certificate storage, did not help either.
The problem happened after the certificate was renewed on the server.
The certificate (*.domain.com) does not exactly match to the server name (mail.domain.com) in k9mail.

[Post 2]:
What algorithms does the server support?
Most likely the answer is very few.
The problem with TLS errors is if the server doesn't like any of the algorithms we send it doesn't give you a helpful error message. So there's nothing we can put to help diagnose the problem.

[Post 3]:
Closing as duplicate of #1789

[Post 4]:
This is the answer I received from the server support, might be useful, so I add it here.

server ... no longer accept SMTP connections except if they are over TLS1.2 (TLS1.1, TLS1.0 as well as any version of SSL are no longer supported; SSL itself is obsolete and insecure and has been superseded by its replacement, TLS, which is an improvement of and development upon SSL - which should no longer be used for security reasons) and use one of the following encryption cypher / hashing algorithm sets :
ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384
ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384
ECDHE-ECDSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305
ECDHE-RSA-CHACHA20-POLY1305
ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256
ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256
ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-SHA384
ECDHE-RSA-AES256-SHA384
ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-SHA256
ECDHE-RSA-AES128-SHA256
Versions of TLS older than 1.2 as well as all versions of SSL are obsolete and should no longer be considered secure or be used.
If your phone or device is running Android Nougat then the issue is with your device's operating system and whatever mail app you try to use will most likely not be able to connect to our server. This is due to Android Nougat not support elliptic curve encryption or only supporting obsolete methods of elliptic curve encryption or something along those lines.
More information on the issue with Android Nougat that's at the heart of this situation can be found at :
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/39133437/sslhandshakeexception-handshake-failed-on-android-n-7-0
and
https://code.google.com/p/android/issues/detail?id=224438 .



## 价值模型参考
以下是可能涉及的价值类型及其定义：
### 人类价值主题 (L2)
- **HV1 (Conformity)**: Adhering to rules, regulations, guidelines, or commonly accepted standards during application use or development.
- **HV2 (Pleasure)**: Providing enjoyment and satisfaction to users when using the application.
- **HV3 (Dignity)**: Maintaining users’ sense of dignity, respect, and personal worth when interacting with the application.
- **HV4 (Inclusiveness)**: Enabling people from different backgrounds, languages, cultures, or knowledge levels to use the application.
- **HV5 (Sense_of_Belonging)**: Supporting users’ feeling of being connected to a group or community through the application.
- **HV6 (Freedom)**: Providing users with options and choices, and avoiding forced dependencies on specific services or workflows.
- **HV7 (Independence)**: Reducing dependency on third-party services or external systems.
- **HV8 (Wealth)**: Considering the monetary or economic cost associated with using the application.
- **HV9 (Privacy)**: Protecting users’ personal information and addressing concerns about data collection or tracking.
- **HV10 (Security)**: Protecting users from external threats or attacks when using the application.

### 系统价值主题 (L3)
- **SV1 (Trust)**: Compelling users to believe that an application is harmless.
- **SV2 (Correctness)**: Ensuring that the application provides expected information and behavior.
- **SV3 (Compatibility)**: Supporting a range of user devices and operating systems.
- **SV4 (Portability)**: Allowing the application to be migrated to other devices.
- **SV5 (Reliability)**: Reducing the occurrence of application failures.
- **SV6 (Efficiency)**: Reducing the time and effort required to perform tasks within the application.
- **SV7 (Energy_Preservation)**: Reducing power or energy consumption during application use.
- **SV8 (Usability)**: Improving the ease of use of the application.
- **SV9 (Accessibility)**: Improving access to information or functionality, particularly for users with special needs.
- **SV10 (Longevity)**: Ensuring prolonged availability of the application in the future.

## 任务
请你以一个软件工程师和项目管理者的视角，回答以下两个问题：

（1）上述Issue讨论是否涉及价值风险？请回答"是"或"否"。

（2）如果涉及价值风险，可能涉及哪些价值风险？请从上述价值模型中选择相关的价值类型，并说明原因。

## 输出格式
请严格按照以下JSON格式输出：
```json
{
  "has_value_risk": true/false,
  "identified_values": [
    {
      "value_id": "价值ID（如HV1, SV1等）",
      "value_name": "价值名称",
      "confidence": 0.0-1.0之间的置信度,
      "reasoning": "识别该价值风险的理由"
    }
  ],
  "overall_analysis": "整体分析说明"
}
```
